---
name: i18n-translation-rules
description: Rules for editing translation JSON files — keep all 4 locales in sync
applyTo: "messages/**/*.json"
---

# Translation File Rules

## Key Synchronization

- **All 4 locale files must have identical key structures**: `fr.json`, `en.json`, `ht.json`, `es.json`
- When adding a new key to any locale file, add the same key to all other locale files immediately
- When removing a key, remove it from all 4 files
- When renaming a key, rename it in all 4 files

## JSON Structure

- Use nested objects to group related translations (e.g., `"nav": { "services": "..." }`)
- Follow the existing nesting pattern — top-level keys match component sections: `nav`, `hero`, `services`, `portfolio`, `process`, `contact`, `footer`
- Keep keys in camelCase: `ctaPrimary`, not `cta-primary` or `CTA_Primary`

## Translation Quality

- `fr` (French): Default locale — formal but warm tone, professional vocabulary
- `en` (English): Professional US English
- `ht` (Haitian Creole): Natural Kreyòl — not literal French translations. Use everyday Haitian expressions
- `es` (Spanish): Standard Latin American Spanish

## Validation

- Every key used in a component via `useTranslations("section")` must exist in all 4 locale files
- No empty string values — every key must have a meaningful translation
- No HTML in translation values — use interpolation via next-intl if needed
